comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: P5 advisory remediation: the {romp-swath-wince, self-waltz-ace} applier.py pair is serialized via a depends_on edge. The {rancid-vane-wreak, veiny-trout-brink} workflow_ops.py pair is NOT edged because a task->story sibling depends_on promotes degenerately (rebar lifts the task to the epic, creating an epic->child self-dependency). That pair is instead serialized by (a) next-batch's file_impact-based conflict-aware scheduling and (b) the Sequencing section in this epic's description.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review gate: PASS (signed, key_id 412fa537, llm_ran=true, 0 blocking). Advisory remediation across 3 review passes: 14 -> 3 advisories. Fixed: P6 ac-quality (removed subjective terms; split compound AC line) now PASSES; added romp-swath-wince->self-waltz-ace ordering edge (applier.py); hardened Bucket-3 AC into per-deliverable checkboxes; added backward-compat + stale-reference-sweep + sequencing sections; added size post-conditions; disambiguated the applier OR-branch. Remaining 3 are accepted: (P5) the veiny-trout/rancid-vane workflow_ops.py pair is sequenced via file_impact + prose since a task->story depends_on promotes degenerately; (E1 0.5) JiraAPIError is fully specified in romp-swath-wince; (COH 0.5) is a positive 'no contradictions' note. SEPARATELY: every review run logs 'container bin [...] FAILED in 0.0s (RuntimeError)' in rebar.llm.plan_review.pass1 — a gate-internal defect under investigation via joe-debug (filed separately).

compiled_state.description:
Tracks remediation of the joe-janitor audit `.joe-janitor/report-2026-06-27.md` (delta vs 2026-06-24). Overall health is strong: no temporal decay, most prior top findings already fixed, security re-verified (semgrep reports zero true positives), and the LLM-disabled isolation story verified empirically. This epic scopes ONLY the requested subset and is decomposed into the 11 child tickets enumerated below.

## Context
Audit headline: the LLM workflow can be disabled / its deps absent and all client-facing ticket workflows still function (verified by a `sys.meta_path` import-blocker experiment); both LLM gates fail closed with actionable errors. The remaining debt is structural; this epic does not attempt to fix every Medium finding.

Deliberately OUT OF SCOPE (left in the report for separate triage): the workflow->plan_review back-edge structural fix, the `review_ticket` legacy/workflow shadow runner, the `drift_refresh` bespoke pass-driver, the `orchestrator.py` re-export shim, the `graph<->_commands` lazy cycle, and the `_engine.py`/`_engine/` name collision.

## Child tickets (decomposition)
- Bucket 1 (low-effort/low-risk wins): `war-bed-plunk` (task) — doc & comment accuracy fixes.
- Bucket 2 (client UX / reliability): `clay-cake-act` (task) — library force_close parity; `veiny-trout-brink` (task) — honor caller LLMConfig in gate ops; `rancid-vane-wreak` (story) — eliminate redundant ticket-graph re-assembly.
- Bucket 3 (top-3 risks, one ticket per deliverable): `romp-swath-wince` (story) — Risk 1 reconciler exception-taxonomy unification (OSS-informed; the child states the `ReconcilerError` base + `RetryExhaustedError(ReconcilerError, RuntimeError)` design); `tarry-creep-range` (task) — Risk 1 prompt write-backend consolidation; `unfed-liner-arson` (story) — Risk 2 split `outbound_differ.py` at the comment-diff seam; `self-waltz-ace` (story) — Risk 2 split `applier.py` at the dispatch/handlers seam; `dazed-daisy-bur` (task) — Risk 2 split `prompts.py` at the front-matter seam; `hush-quail-holm` (story) — Risk 3 decompose `reconcile_once()` in-place.
- Bucket 4 (E2E config tests): `foamy-gem-skip` (story) — close the F1/F2/F3 gaps.

## Bucket 4 gap definitions (F1/F2/F3)
- F1: the completion close gate is fail-closed-tested only via the library, not driven over CLI/MCP.
- F2: the completion fail-closed path is only simulated (monkeypatched error), never run with the real `[agents]` extra absent in the lean-install lane.
- F3: the optionality per-extra CI matrix omits `mcp` and `grounding`.

## Backward-compatibility / reversibility (one-way-door review)
- Bucket 2 `force_close` is ADDITIVE (a new optional parameter) — non-breaking. Honoring the caller's LLMConfig fixes a documented divergence no consumer relies on; the change is forward-only and covered by the child's parity test.
- Risk 1 exception unification is designed backward-COMPATIBLE: the unified `RetryExhaustedError(ReconcilerError, RuntimeError)` is still caught by every existing `except RuntimeError` and `except Exception` site (zero call-site breakage), so no expand-contract migration is needed. The `romp-swath-wince` child ticket records that taxonomy contract and serves as the design record (ADR-equivalent) for the one-way-door decision.
- All file splits preserve public imports via re-export shims; a stale-reference sweep is required (see Success Criteria + AC).

## Sequencing (de-risking, MVP-first)
Land ONE risky Bucket-3 seam end-to-end first as the de-risking slice — recommended `dazed-daisy-bur` (prompts.py split: simplest, exercises the split -> re-export -> allowlist-drop -> green-CI loop) — before the larger `outbound_differ.py`/`applier.py` splits. Ordering edges are recorded for the two file-conflict pairs: `romp-swath-wince` precedes `self-waltz-ace` (both touch `applier.py`; the split must land on the unified exception type), and `rancid-vane-wreak` precedes `veiny-trout-brink` (both touch `workflow_ops.py`/`production_batch_runner.py`).

## Success Criteria
- All 11 child tickets close through the completion-verifier gate.
- The optionality.yml lean-install lane stays green and semgrep continues to report zero true positives (no security regression).
- Every large-file split lands along the LOGICAL SEAMS named in its child ticket; no new file under 100 LOC; no mechanical line-count splitting.
- No stale references (imports, docs, ADRs) to the pre-split module layout survive the `outbound_differ.py`/`applier.py`/`prompts.py` splits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bucket 1 `war-bed-plunk`: doc/comment accuracy fixes merged; no stale `ticket_reducer/`/`ticket_graph/` path remains (grep verifies).
- [ ] Bucket 2a `clay-cake-act`: library `force_close` reaches parity with the CLI (additive, non-breaking); a parity test asserts identical behavior.
- [ ] Bucket 2b `veiny-trout-brink`: the caller's explicit LLMConfig is reflected in the verdict's model/runner fields; the divergence note is removed.
- [ ] Bucket 2c `rancid-vane-wreak`: a single plan-review run assembles the ticket graph at most once (asserted by a read-counter spy); verdict bytes unchanged.
- [ ] Bucket 3 Risk-1a `romp-swath-wince`: one `RetryExhaustedError`/`JiraAPIError` type, caught by `except RuntimeError`/`except Exception`/`except ReconcilerError`; both raise sites chain the cause.
- [ ] Bucket 3 Risk-1b `tarry-creep-range`: every prompt write goes through the single atomic writer under one id rule; no `write_text` write path remains.
- [ ] Bucket 3 Risk-2a `unfed-liner-arson`: `outbound_differ.py` split at the comment-diff seam; drops OFF `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` (below the 800 cap); the extracted `outbound_comments.py` sibling is >= 100 LOC.
- [ ] Bucket 3 Risk-2b `self-waltz-ace`: `applier.py` dispatch/handlers split; `_apply_batch` nesting <= 4; PRIMARY end-state: `applier.py` drops below 800 LOC, removing it from the allowlist. Fallback only if the seam cannot reach <800: the allowlist entry + architecture-doc remedy row are updated to match the new structure, with the reason stated in the PR.
- [ ] Bucket 3 Risk-2c `dazed-daisy-bur`: `prompts.py` front-matter cluster extracted to a >= 100-LOC sibling; drops OFF the allowlist; public symbols remain importable from `prompts.py`.
- [ ] Bucket 3 Risk-3 `hush-quail-holm`: `reconcile_once()` is a thin sequencer over in-file phase helpers; at least the load/persist phases gain direct unit tests.
- [ ] Bucket 4 `foamy-gem-skip`: F1 (CLI+MCP close gate), F2 (real-absence test in the lean-install lane), F3 (`mcp`+`grounding` in the per-extra matrix) covered with no duplication of gate-logic tests; `test_parity.py` untouched.
- [ ] Constraint at epic close: the optionality.yml lean-install lane is green.
- [ ] Constraint at epic close: semgrep reports zero true positives (no security regression).
- [ ] Constraint at epic close: every newly created file is >= 100 LOC (no sub-floor split).
- [ ] Constraint at epic close: no stale references to pre-split module paths survive (grep verifies).
compiled_state.title: Codebase-health remediation — 2026-06-27 audit
